name: Caramel Nut Frosting
recipeInstructions:
1. Place 1-1/2 cups of the sugar into a heavy saucepan, taking care that no crystals cling to the sides of the pan. Add syrup.
2. Carefully add cream down the sides of the pan to wash away any crystals that may have collected there.
3. Place the saucepan over medium heat and stir until the sugar melts.
4. When the mixture boils, place the remaining sugar in a heavy saute pan (preferably cast iron) over medium heat and stir until it melts and turns golden brown. Do not let the sugar burn or it will taste bitter.
5. Add the browned sugar to the mixture in the saucepan. Stir and cook until the mixture reaches the soft ball stage (240°F).
6. Add vanilla and pour the mixture over chunked butter in a mixing bowl. Let cool for about 5 minutes, then beat vigorously until the frosting reaches spreading consistency.
7. Add chopped pecans. Frost the top of each layer, stacking one upon the other, then frost the side of the cake. Freezes well [but I'll never know].
